content.verdict: Longitude 131° is the only luxury lodging with direct, unobstructed views of Uluru — 16 luxury tented pavilions on a sand dune, each facing the rock from bed. Baillie Lodges' flagship in the Red Centre. All-inclusive (meals, premium drinks, spa, all signature experiences including Field of Light access and Tabletop dune-top dining). The single most architecturally significant honeymoon site in Australia after qualia.
content.best_room: All 16 tented pavilions face Uluru — there are no inferior rooms. The Dune Pavilion (1 unit, $5,500+/night) is the upgrade — larger footprint, private plunge pool, sunken lounge. Otherwise, request a centrally-positioned tent (Tent 8 or 9) for the best symmetry to Uluru.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Ayers Rock airport
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into AYQ (direct from Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane). 5-min hotel transfer. Welcome with sparkling and warm towels at the central lodge. Settle in. Sunset Uluru viewing from your private deck with canapés. Dinner at Dune House — kangaroo carpaccio, native finger lime, Penfolds Grange.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunrise Uluru base walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn coffee on your deck as the rock turns red. Guided base walk (10km, 3.5h) around the entire perimeter of Uluru with an Anangu cultural interpreter. Lunch at the lodge. Afternoon Spa Kinara hot stone massage. Dinner at Dune House.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Field of Light + Tabletop dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Bruce Munro's installation of 50,000 illuminated stems across the desert at dusk. Champagne in the dunes as the field lights up. Then to Tabletop — the lodge's signature dune-top fine-dining experience under the southern stars. The most photographed honeymoon meal in Australia.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kata Tjuta hike + helicopter return
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 1h to Kata Tjuta (the Olgas). Walk of the Winds gorge hike (3h). Helicopter scenic flight back to Uluru — viewing both monoliths from air. Lunch on return. Spa afternoon. Dinner at Dune House.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Camel sunrise + Anangu cultural day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Camel-train ride along the dunes at sunrise. Mid-morning visit to the Cultural Centre and Mutitjulu Waterhole with Anangu Indigenous guide — dot-painting workshop, bush-tucker tasting. Late lunch at the lodge. Aboriginal-themed dinner with elder storytelling.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sounds of Silence dinner alternative or rest day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Either rest at the pool with a champagne lunch, or join the Sounds of Silence multi-course outback dinner (separate from Tabletop, more communal experience). Both end with star-talker astronomy session.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final sunrise & departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final sunrise over Uluru from your bed. Slow breakfast on the deck — gluten-free pastries, Mungalli yoghurt, native quandong jam. 5-min transfer to AYQ for return flight.

caveats: Desert climate: 35°C+ summer days, 5°C winter nights — pack layers either season.
All-inclusive but helicopter, camel rides, and some signature experiences are still add-ons.
16 tents only — books out 9–12 months ahead for May–September.
Walking distances around the lodge include sand sections; flat shoes preferred.
Dust storms occasional in summer; tents are sealed but pack tissues.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the unobstructed Uluru views from bed, all-inclusive luxury, Tabletop dune-top dining, and the spiritual significance of the location create a once-in-a-lifetime honeymoon experience.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May–September (cool desert dry season, 18–25°C days, low humidity). Avoid Dec–February searing heat (40°C+) unless desert summer is your specific request.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 9–12 months for Dune Pavilion or peak May-Sept dates. Field of Light and Tabletop need separate confirmation slots.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, but children under 10 are rare; the lodge demographic is firmly adult-couple.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Dune Pavilion for the private plunge pool. Otherwise any centrally-located tent (8 or 9) for the cleanest Uluru sight-line.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly directly to Ayers Rock (AYQ) from Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane (direct, 3-3.5h). 5-min lodge transfer. Helicopter charter from Alice Springs available.
